title: Pro-Rata Rights Agreement
description: Confirms an investor's right to maintain their percentage ownership in future priced funding rounds.
whatsCovered: Definition of investor pro-rata allocation options
Notice timeline for new funding priced rounds
Exclusions for employee stock pools and conversions
Termination of rights upon IPO or acquisition
whenToUse: Use this agreement to grant early-stage investors the contractual option to participate in future Series A, B, and C pricing rounds.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: PRO-RATA RIGHTS AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Pro-Rata Rights Agreement. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Pro-Rata Dilution Protections
clauses.body: Investor {investor_name} is granted pro-rata rights to buy shares in future funding rounds. Notice of new rounds must be given at least {notice_days} days before closing.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Why do investors want pro-rata rights?
faq.answer: It protects them from dilution, allowing them to purchase enough shares in later rounds to keep their ownership percentage constant.

faq.question: When do pro-rata rights expire?
faq.answer: Typically, they terminate immediately prior to the closing of the company's Initial Public Offering (IPO) or a major acquisition.
